title: Sour Flaky Nostalgic Canadian Biryani Stir-fry
description: A vibrant and tangy take on the classic biryani masala combined with a flaky crust, infused with Canadian spices and cured meats for added depth of flavor. Perfect as an appetizer or side dish to satisfy any craving for a deliciously rich, yet subtly sour meal.

ingredients.name: 1 cup rice blend (e.g. basmati or jasmine)
ingredients.notes: Use a mix of short and long grains for texture contrast.

instructions: Heat the ghee in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the onions and cook until translucent, then add garlic and sauté for another minute.
Add green bell pepper and zucchini, cook until vegetables are soft and tender but still crisp.
Stir in cumin seeds and turmeric; cook for about 30 seconds.
Mix in lemon juice and sour cream (if using), stirring to combine evenly with the vegetables. Season with salt to taste.
To serve, spoon the stir-fry mixture over a bed of fragrant rice blend that has been cooked separately until fluffy but still slightly firm.
Optional: Garnish with finely chopped fresh herbs like cilantro or parsley for added freshness.
